articles.summary: 'Shutdown': Moody's expects Dubai hotel occupancy to plummet to 10 percent The US-based financial analysis firm Moody's predicts that hotel occupancy in Dubai is set to plummet to 10 percent from a pre-war figure of 80 percent in the second quarter of the year, which ends on 1 July, The Wall Street Journal reported on Wednesday. "This represents an effective shutdown of large parts of the hospitality sector," Moody’s said. articles.title: Iran says ceasefire and ending war top priorities in talks
articles.summary: Iran says ceasefire and ending war top priorities in talks Iran’s foreign ministry spokesperson, Esmaeil Baghaei, says current discussions centre on securing a ceasefire, ending the war and restoring regional stability. In an interview with IRNA, Baghaei said officials in Tehran are still examining the US proposal and will decide once the review is complete. He added that Iran will relay its decision to Pakistani mediators, with the next steps to depend on the outcome of the ongoing exchange of messages.
